description: Dismissing errors as pre-existing instead of fixing them
Silently catching exceptions with empty catch blocks
Adding TODO or FIXME comments instead of solving the problem now
Writing code that assumes happy path without handling failure cases
Leaving known broken state because 'it's not my change'
Returning early or skipping logic when an unexpected condition is hit instead of handling it
Deferring error handling to the caller without documenting or enforcing it
Using fallback values that mask failures silently (returning empty string, null, undefined on error)
Noting a problem in prose but not acting on it in code
Blaming the environment or dependencies instead of working around or fixing the issue
Identifying architectural inefficiencies but implementing workarounds instead of fixing the root cause
Documenting a known dangerous state and designing elaborate workarounds instead of fixing the root cause
